name: Catholic Charities Housing Services
description_short: Catholic Charities Housing Services provides free and low-cost assistance with housing, behavioral health, and basic needs across 17 Washington counties for vulnerable populations.
description_long:
Catholic Charities Serving Central Washington has operated for over 75 years as a mission-driven nonprofit providing life-affirming services to vulnerable populations regardless of faith or immigration status. The organization serves nearly 65,000 people annually across 17 Washington counties, with a stated commitment to building a more just and equitable world by addressing systemic poverty and inequality.

Catholic Charities Housing Services offers comprehensive housing and homeless support alongside behavioral health counseling, early learning programs, pregnancy and parenting services, youth and young adult support, and senior services. Their housing-specific offerings include affordable housing development and homeless support services. Beyond housing, they provide mental health crisis de-escalation techniques, family counseling, children's behavioral therapy (including ABA for autism spectrum disorder), adult counseling, and specialized programs like First Episode Psychosis intervention and Assertive Community Treatment for chronically mentally ill adults.

The organization distinguishes itself through integrated wraparound services that address multiple needs simultaneously—combining housing assistance with mental health support rather than treating them separately. They explicitly serve all populations regardless of citizenship or immigration status, and offer programs spanning the entire lifespan from prenatal care through senior services. Their St. Vincent Center provides material assistance including clothing vouchers, hygiene kits, and baby items alongside housing and counseling services.

As a free-help nonprofit, Catholic Charities does not generate revenue from clients and operates on a mission-based model. However, the website provides limited specific details about eligibility requirements, wait times, service capacity, or current availability in all 17 counties. Prospective clients would need to contact the organization directly for concrete information about accessing specific services and any potential geographic or demographic limitations.
pros: Serves all ages from prenatal through seniors with specialized programs for each life stage
Explicitly serves people regardless of citizenship, immigration status, or religious background
Integrated wraparound services combining housing assistance with mental health and behavioral support
Operates across 17 Washington counties with significant reach (serves ~65,000 people annually)
Specialized mental health programs including First Episode Psychosis intervention and ABA therapy for autism
Provides material assistance (clothing vouchers, hygiene kits, baby items) alongside counseling and housing
75-year organizational track record with established credibility and community presence
cons: Website lacks specific eligibility criteria, making it unclear who qualifies for services
No information about wait times, service capacity, or current availability in all 17 counties served
Limited details about housing services specifically—affordable housing programs mentioned but not explained
Geographic coverage spans 17 counties, potentially requiring significant travel for some clients
No online application, intake form, or clear contact mechanism for requesting specific services
best_for: Low-income families with children needing behavioral health and housing support simultaneously
Unhoused or at-risk individuals in Central Washington seeking emergency and long-term housing assistance
Parents seeking low-cost counseling, parenting support, or early childhood development services
Adults and youth experiencing mental health crises or chronic mental illness needing wraparound care
diagnosis: Catholic Charities Housing Services is best for low-income individuals and families in Central Washington seeking integrated housing and mental health support on a completely free or low-cost basis. The main caveat is that the website provides minimal detail about specific eligibility, capacity, and availability—direct contact with the organization is necessary to determine if you qualify and when services can be accessed.
services: Behavioral health and mental health counseling for all ages
Applied Behavioral Analysis (ABA) for autism spectrum disorder (ages 2-5)
First Episode Psychosis intervention program for youth and young adults
Program for Assertive Community Treatment (PACT) for chronically mentally ill adults
Wraparound with Intensive Services (WISe) for young children
Clothing vouchers and hygiene kits (St. Vincent Center)
Affordable housing development and placement services
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 5301 Tieton Dr, Suite G, Yakima, WA 98908
